sanskrit:
अन्नाद्भवन्ति भूतानि पर्जन्यादन्नसम्भवः।
यज्ञाद्भवति पर्जन्यो यज्ञः कर्मसमुद्भवः॥
transliteration:
annād bhavanti bhūtāni parjanyād anna-sambhavaḥ |
yajñād bhavati parjanyo yajñaḥ karma-samudbhavaḥ ||
literal_meaning: From food beings arise; from rain food is produced; from sacrifice comes rain; sacrifice is born of action.
contextual_meaning: Human well-being is embedded in a circular ecological-ethical economy: bodily life depends on food, food on rain, rain on cooperative action (yajña), and yajña on purposeful work. Psychologically, it asserts that personal sustenance is never isolated; it is contingent on reciprocal duty and cosmic cooperation.
psychological_state: Cognitive reframing from egocentric 'I provide for myself' to systemic 'I participate in a give-receive cycle', reducing existential anxiety and narcissistic self-sufficiency.
trigger_scenario: Standing in a supermarket queue complaining about prices, then realizing every item exists because of soil, farmers, rainfall, and countless unseen contributors.
anchor_text: Your meal depends on the shared work of earth, rain, and human hands.
